title: lonicera etrusca var. cyrenaica
intro: Lonicera etrusca var. cyrenaica, also known as the Etruscan honeysuckle or Italian honeysuckle, is a deciduous climbing shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant white or pink flowers and edible red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Lonicera etrusca var. cyrenaica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
